LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE SEP-30-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GRAMMARLY, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GRAMMARLY, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF PLAINTIFF LAURA HIBBEN'S CLAIMS IS GRANTED AND THE MATTER IS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Off Calendar May-21-2026 Continued To Nov-04-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Continued To Jun-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GRAMMARLY, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Grammarly, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration of plaintiff Laura Hibben's claims is granted and the matter is stayed pending arbitration (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Sep-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GRAMMARLY, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to admit Serine Consolino pro hac vice is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
